prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene (21 atoms: 10 C, 10 H, 1 Fe) using GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Finite-difference Fukui indices computed from Mulliken partial charges on the N−1, N, and N+1 charge states (cation: +1/doublet; anion: −1/doublet). The iron center (atom 11) is the most electrophilic site (f⁺ = 0.22885456) and the most nucleophilic site (f⁻ = 0.25938191), with f⁰ = 0.24411824 and dual descriptor = −0.030527354. The negative dual descriptor on Fe confirms its nucleophilic character. Carbon ring atoms carry f⁺ ≈ 0.01364, f⁻ ≈ 0.01218; hydrogen atoms carry f⁺ ≈ 0.06348, f⁻ ≈ 0.06188. Both Cp rings are equivalent by symmetry. Integrity: trustworthy=true; sum(f⁺)=1.0000, sum(f−)=1.0000. No warnings reported.
